theory.intro: A unit fraction has a numerator of $1$. You can decide whether it is smaller or larger than $\tfrac{1}{2}$ by looking at its denominator.
facts: $\tfrac{1}{2}$ splits one whole into **two** equal parts.
A bigger denominator means **more, smaller** parts — so the part is **less** than $\tfrac{1}{2}$.
$\tfrac{1}{2}$ is the **only** unit fraction equal to a half; $\tfrac{1}{3}, \tfrac{1}{4}, \dots$ are all smaller.
steps: Look at the denominator of the unit fraction.
If the denominator is more than $2$, the fraction is **less** than $\tfrac{1}{2}$.
Compare to $\tfrac{1}{2}$: $\tfrac{1}{2}$ is the largest unit fraction except $1$ itself.
